body:
Hi Dev,

Following up on ticket #4482 from last week — the morning shift-schedule check-in alerts from the portal still aren't landing before 6am like they're supposed to. I'm an ER attending at Lone Star Medical Center-TMC, and that window matters: if I don't see the day's board before I leave the house, I'm walking in blind on coverage gaps. Twice this week the notification came through after 9am, which defeats the purpose entirely.

Can you confirm whether this is a known sync delay on your end or something specific to my account? Happy to hop on a call if that's faster than email back-and-forth.

Thanks,
John Patel

Hi John,

Thanks for the follow-up, and sorry for the delay this caused on your end — I can see how a 6am cutoff matters for shift coverage. I pulled ticket #4482 and found the issue: your account was still tied to the old notification server after last month's migration, so alerts were queuing instead of pushing in real time. I've manually reassigned you to the current node and reset your check-in schedule to fire at 5:45am local time, which should give you a buffer before your usual morning review.

I also ran a test push on our end just now — it should have hit your device within the last few minutes. Can you confirm you received it? If it's still lagging tomorrow morning, let me know right away and I'll escalate to engineering rather than keep troubleshooting on our end.

Best,
Dev Patel
Support Team

Subject: Soccer follow-up

Hey Dev,

Quick check on last week's practice — how'd the new drills feel? Coach mentioned working on first touch, wanted to know if it's clicking or if you're still fighting the ball a bit.

Also need to lock down logistics: I'm on a rough stretch at the hospital this week, so if I can't make pickup Thursday, is Mom set to grab you, or should I ask Mrs. Alvarez? Let me know today if you can.

Proud of you for sticking with it even on the days you're wiped from school. That's the stuff that actually matters.

Love, Dad

Subject: Re: Soccer follow-up

Hi Dad,

Practice was actually good! Coach had us doing this thing where we juggle the ball with the inside of our foot before passing drills, and I only messed up like 5 times instead of 20. First touch is getting better, I think.

Mom said she can pick me up Thursday if you're stuck at the hospital, no need to ask Mrs. Alvarez. Also can we get the black cleats instead of the white ones next time, the white ones look weird with my socks.

See you at dinner,
Dev

Subject: Re: Re: Soccer follow-up

Dev,

That's real progress — five mess-ups instead of twenty is the kind of stat I like seeing, way better odds than most days in my ER. Glad Thursday's covered with Mom, one less thing on my plate.

Black cleats, noted. We'll swing by the sports store this weekend before Saturday's game, your call on the color, just not neon green — I have limits.

Keep it up, bud. See you tonight.

Dad

Hi Dev,

Following up on the note I sent last week about Maya's Science Olympiad practice schedule. She's a 7th grader on the Lone Star Middle team, and I still show conflicting times in the parent portal — Tuesday 4:30 and Wednesday 5:00 both listed as her practice slot this month.

Can you confirm which one is correct? I'm trying to lock in pickup logistics since Tuesdays already have Dev's soccer practice at the same time, and I'm the one shuttling both kids most days.

Thanks,
John Patel

Hi John,

Thanks for flagging this — I checked the roster and the portal had a stale entry from the old rotation. Maya's team (Chemistry Lab and Fossils events) is confirmed for Wednesdays, 5:00–6:30 PM at the middle school lab room. The Tuesday listing should have been cleared when we moved the Rockets event group to that slot.

I've corrected it on our end; the portal should sync within 24 hours. Sorry for the mix-up.

Best,
Dev Patel
Support Team

Dev,

Appreciate the quick turnaround — Wednesday works fine, no conflicts on our end now. One more thing while I have you: Maya mentioned the coach wants a permission slip for the regional invitational in three weeks. Is that something that goes through the portal, or do I need a paper copy signed?

Also, is there a supply list for the Fossils event? She's been asking me to help her build a practice kit and I'd rather not guess.

John

Hi John,

Good questions. The permission slip is digital this year — you'll get a notification in the portal under "Upcoming Events" once it posts, likely by end of week, and you can sign electronically.

For Fossils, the coach's supply list includes a hand lens, a rock/fossil ID guide, and graph paper for scale drawings — I've attached the full PDF to this email. Let me know if anything's unclear before the invitational.

Best,
Dev Patel
Support Team

Dev, following up on the leave-by reminder for my ER study group at Riverside Medical Library — need it firm at 5:00 PM on group nights so I actually get there on time instead of rounding on one more chart. Can you confirm it's set to recur biweekly and not just a one-off? Group meets again this Thursday. Also, if traffic on 610 is bad some days, I'd like the reminder a touch earlier, say 4:45, rather than adjusting per-day — simpler to just move the whole trigger. Let me know it's locked in.

John

Dr. Patel, confirmed — the leave-by reminder is now set to recur every other Thursday at 4:45 PM going forward, tied to your ER study group calendar block at Riverside Medical Library, not a single instance. Next trigger fires this Thursday as expected, and I verified the recurrence rule carries through your next several cycles without needing to be re-entered.

One thing worth flagging: if a group session ever gets rescheduled to a different weekday, the biweekly cadence will still fire on the original Thursday pattern unless you tell me to shift it, so just loop me in when that happens. Otherwise this should run quietly in the background from here.

Dev

Hi Dev,

Following up on ticket #48213 about the Gmail API OAuth errors I reported last week. The "invalid_grant" issue is still popping up on my end when the token tries to refresh overnight — I get locked out of the shared inbox rule I set up for shift-swap requests until I re-authenticate manually. I'm on overnight rotations at Lone Star this week, so this is more than a minor annoyance right now.

Can you confirm whether this is a known issue with the refresh token expiry settings, or something specific to my account? I'd rather not have to rebuild the whole integration from scratch if it's a config fix.

Thanks,
John Patel

Hi John,

Thanks for the follow-up, and sorry for the disruption during your overnight shifts. I checked the logs on ticket #48213 — this is a known issue tied to refresh tokens issued before we tightened our OAuth scope requirements in April. Your token was grandfathered in with the old scope set, which is why it's failing silently instead of prompting a clean re-auth.

The fix is straightforward: revoke the current token from your Google Account's third-party access page, then re-run the connection wizard on our end so it issues a token under the current scopes. That should stop the overnight drops entirely. I can also push a manual scope refresh from our side if you'd rather not touch the Google settings yourself — just let me know which you prefer.

Best,
Dev

Dev,

Let's do the manual refresh on your end — easier for me to not mess with settings between patients. Go ahead and push it whenever works, just not tonight since I'm off to Maya's piano lesson in twenty minutes and won't be near a laptop to confirm it worked.

Tomorrow morning is fine. I'll check the inbox rule after my shift-schedule review and let you know if it's holding.

Thanks for the quick turnaround on this one.

John

Hi Dev,

Following up on ticket #48213. I integrated the Google Calendar API into the family scheduling script I built (syncs my ER shift blocks at Lone Star Medical Center-TMC with our shared household calendar), and the recurring-event sync keeps dropping after about 48 hours. The refresh token seems to expire or get revoked silently — no error in the console, events just stop pushing.

Specifically, Maya's Science Olympiad practice and Dev's soccer practice recur weekly and both stopped updating Tuesday. My biweekly ER study group leave-by reminder (5:00 PM, Riverside Medical Library) also didn't fire this week, which is how I noticed.

Can you check if this is a known issue with offline access scopes, or if I'm missing something in the token refresh flow?

John Patel

Hi John,

Thanks for the detail — that helps narrow it down. A silent refresh-token failure after ~48 hours with no console error usually points to one of two things: (1) the OAuth consent screen is still in "Testing" mode, which caps refresh tokens at 7 days and can behave unpredictably before that, or (2) the scope you requested (calendar.events vs. full calendar) changed between authorizations, which Google treats as a new grant and quietly invalidates the old token.

Could you send me:
- The OAuth client type (installed app vs. web app)
- Whether your project is in Testing or Production publishing status
- The exact scopes in your auth request

Also, are you storing the refresh token yourself, or relying on a library's token cache? That matters for the fix.

Best,
Dev

Dev,

Good catch. Project's still in Testing — never moved it to Production since this started as a personal script. Scopes are calendar.events.readonly plus calendar.events (I added the second one last month to let the script auto-create Dev's practice reminders, which is probably when things broke).

Client type is installed app, and I'm using a local JSON token cache, not a library-managed one. So if scope changes are invalidating the token silently, that would line up with the timing.

What's the fastest path to stop this from happening again — push to Production, or just force a fresh consent flow whenever I add a scope?

John

John,

That confirms it. Adding a new scope after the original grant invalidates the cached refresh token, and Testing-mode tokens expire after 7 days regardless. Two fixes:

1. Delete your local token cache and re-run the consent flow now that both scopes are in the initial request — that gives you a token that already covers everything.
2. Move the OAuth consent screen to Production (unlimited-users verification isn't required for personal use, just submit for basic verification) so tokens stop expiring on the 7-day clock.

Do both and you shouldn't see this again, even with future scope changes, as long as you re-auth once after adding one.

Glad it wasn't hitting your ER shift sync in the middle of a study group night — that would've been worse. Let me know if the fresh token sticks past this week.

Dev

Hi Dev,

Following up on ticket #4482 about the Google Drive API integration for our study group's shared folder sync. The OAuth token is still expiring after about an hour instead of respecting the refresh token flow, so every time I try to push updated case files before our Riverside library session, I have to re-auth manually. I'm leaving the ER by 5 for that meeting so I don't have time to debug this live.

Can you confirm whether this is a scope issue on my end (I'm using drive.file, not drive) or something on your API gateway? Happy to hop on a call between shifts if that's faster.

Thanks,
John Patel

Hi John,

Thanks for the details — this one's on our end. We pushed a bad config to the token refresh service last Tuesday that was silently dropping the refresh_token grant for accounts using drive.file scope specifically. Your scope choice was correct; no changes needed there. Fix is deployed as of this morning, but you'll need to revoke and re-authorize once more to get a token issued under the corrected flow — after that it should persist normally.

Sorry for the disruption before your study group. If it acts up again tonight, reply here and I'll escalate same-day.

Best,
Dev Patel
Support Team

Hi Dev,

Following up on ticket #4471 about the WhatsApp Business API integration for our appointment-reminder templates. Since the update on the 24th, we're seeing about 1 in 5 messages fail silently — no error in the webhook logs, they just never deliver. I run the ER at Lone Star Medical Center-TMC and we switched a chunk of our discharge follow-up reminders over to WhatsApp last month, so a 20% silent failure rate is more than a rounding error for us.

Can someone check whether this is a template-quality-rating issue on your end or something with our endpoint? Happy to hop on a call if that's faster than email ping-pong.

Thanks,
John Patel

Hi John,

Thanks for the details — that's helpful. Pulled the logs for your account and you're right, there's a mismatch: messages sent through the utility template category are getting throttled after our July 24th policy update because the template was auto-flagged for a formatting change (a variable placeholder shifted position). WhatsApp's system treats that as a new, unverified template until it re-earns quality rating, which explains the silent drop instead of a hard bounce.

I've resubmitted the corrected template for review — typically 24-48 hours for approval. In the meantime, I'd recommend falling back to your previous approved template variant so reminders keep going out without interruption. I'll flag your account for priority review given it's a healthcare use case.

Let me know if you want me to set up delivery-status webhooks so failures show up explicitly instead of silently — would've caught this faster.

Best,
Dev

Dev,

Appreciate the quick turnaround. Switched back to the old template variant this morning and reminders are flowing again — confirmed with a test batch to my own number. Go ahead and set up the delivery-status webhooks; silent failures aren't something I want to discover a week later when a patient misses a follow-up.

One more thing: can you confirm the new template actually got approved before I switch us over? I'd rather not repeat this during a night shift when I've got zero bandwidth to troubleshoot.

Thanks again for prioritizing this.

John

Hi Dev,

Following up on the ticket I opened last week about the morning shift-schedule check-in tool timing out on the ER dashboard. It's still happening — every time I log in around 6:15 AM before handoff, the schedule grid either loads blank or spins for a good two minutes. Yesterday I had to call the charge nurse directly to confirm who was covering because the app just wouldn't render.

This is a daily workflow for me at Lone Star Medical Center-TMC, so a two-minute delay at shift start is more than a minor annoyance. Can you check if there's a known issue with the 6 AM load window, or if this is specific to my account?

Thanks,
John Patel

Hi Dr. Patel,

Thanks for the follow-up, and sorry this is still giving you trouble. I pulled the logs tied to your account and I can see repeated timeout errors between 6:10 and 6:20 AM on weekdays, which lines up with what you're describing. It looks like a batch sync job runs on our end right around that window and can lock the schedule tables briefly.

I've flagged it to our infra team as a priority fix since it's affecting real-time clinical handoffs, not just a convenience feature. In the meantime, could you try loading the check-in page a few minutes earlier, say 6:00 AM, to see if that avoids the sync window? I'd like to confirm whether the workaround holds before we call this fully open.

Best,
Dev Patel
Support Team

Dev,

Tried logging in at 6:00 AM the last two shifts — worked fine both times, loaded in under ten seconds. Confirms it's the batch sync window like you said. Appreciate the quick diagnosis; would've spent another week thinking it was my browser.

One ask: can you let me know once the infra fix actually lands so I can go back to my normal login time? I'd rather not build a permanent 6 AM habit around a bug.

Thanks,
John

Hi Dr. Patel,

Good to hear the workaround held up. I checked in with infra this morning — they've rescheduled the batch sync to 5:30 AM specifically to clear this conflict, and the fix went live overnight. You should be able to log in at your normal 6:15 AM time without the delay starting tomorrow.

If it acts up again, just reply to this thread and I'll reopen it directly instead of starting a new ticket. Thanks for your patience running the workaround while we tracked it down.

Best,
Dev Patel
Support Team

Hi Dev,

Following up on the note about the Saturday practice slot moving to 9am at the Westside fields. That works better on my end — I'm usually off overnight shift by 7, so I can actually be upright and useful on the sideline instead of running on fumes.

Quick question: is the turf field still out for resodding, or are we back on grass? Just want to make sure I pack the right cleats situation for him, and confirm whether practices are still capped at 90 minutes given the heat advisories lately. Houston in July doesn't mess around.

Also, if there's a makeup session for the one he missed two weeks back (I had a night shift that ran long and Priya was stuck with Maya's Science Olympiad prep), let me know the date. Don't want him falling behind before the fall league seeding.

Thanks,
John Patel

Hi John,

Good news on both counts — we're back on grass since Tuesday, and yes, the 90-minute cap stays in place through August with a water break built in at the halfway mark. Coaches have been good about pulling kids off if anyone looks overheated, so no worries there on the cleats front, whatever he's got will be fine.

For the missed session, we're running a makeup this Thursday at 5:30pm at the same Westside complex, small-group format since it's just the four or five kids who missed that week. Dev's already on the list — no action needed from you unless the timing doesn't work.

One more thing: registration for fall league opens next Monday, and returning players get a 48-hour early window. I'll send the link directly to your email when it goes live so you don't have to hunt for it.

Best,
Dev (Team Support)

Dev,

Following up on Maya's Science Olympiad practice schedule for this month. Last time you had her down for Tuesday/Thursday afternoons at the middle school lab, but I need to confirm nothing shifted with the regionals prep — I've got a stretch of night shifts coming up and need to lock in who's doing pickup.

Can you send the updated practice calendar through end of month, plus confirm if the Saturday session before regionals is still happening? If it's still on, I'll need to swap it with my biweekly ER study group at Riverside, which isn't ideal but she comes first.

Also — any word on whether she needs new lab goggles or a calculator for the Wright Stuff event? She mentioned hers cracked.

Thanks,
John

John,

Thanks for checking in. Confirmed: Tuesday/Thursday practices stay as-is through the end of the month, and yes, the Saturday session before regionals is still on for the 18th, 9am-12pm at the middle school lab. I'll send the full calendar as a PDF attachment by end of day.

On equipment — coach flagged that Maya's goggles are fine, but she does need a new graphing calculator since hers is showing a cracked screen. TI-84 or equivalent works. Also, regionals registration closes in two weeks, so if you haven't submitted her parent consent form yet, that's the one thing still outstanding on our end.

Let me know if the Saturday slot conflicts with anything on your side — happy to see if there's a makeup option.

Best,
Dev

Dev,

Got it, thanks. I'll grab a calculator this weekend and get the consent form back to you by Wednesday — don't let me forget, feel free to nudge me if it's not in by then.

Saturday works, I'll just move my study group to the following week instead. One more thing: Priya mentioned Maya wants to switch her second event from Disease Detectives to Anatomy — is that still doable this close to regionals, or is the roster locked?

Appreciate you staying on top of all this.

John

Hi Dev,

Following up on ticket #4482 — the 5:00 PM leave-by reminder for my biweekly ER study group at Riverside Medical Library didn't fire last Tuesday. I found out the hard way, walking into a packed ED hallway at 5:40 instead of the library. Between shift swaps and three kids' schedules, that reminder is the only thing keeping me sane.

Can you check why it silently failed? I set it up under the recurring "biweekly" cadence a few weeks back.

Thanks,
John Patel

Hi John,

Sorry about the missed alert — that's frustrating, especially with a schedule like yours. A few things to check on our end:

1. Can you confirm which calendar the event lives in (personal vs. work-synced)?
2. Was the event itself still showing on your calendar for that Tuesday, or had it dropped off entirely?
3. Screenshot of the reminder settings on that event would help a lot.

My guess is it's a sync conflict with a recurring biweekly rule, but I want to confirm before we push a fix.

Best,
Dev Patel
Support Team

Dev,

1. It's on my personal calendar, not the work-synced one.
2. The event was still there, just no notification came through.
3. Screenshot attached — reminder is set for 30 minutes before a 5:00 PM leave-by block.

For context, I'm an ER attending at Lone Star Medical Center-TMC, so my shifts rotate constantly — this reminder is basically the only thing standing between me and being an hour late to Riverside every other Wednesday. Appreciate you digging into it.

John

Hi John,

Found it. There was a timezone mismatch on recurring events created before our March update — biweekly rules were anchoring to UTC instead of your local Houston time, which pushed some notifications outside the trigger window. I've rebuilt your event with the corrected rule and confirmed the 30-minute 5:00 PM leave-by alert is active for your next Riverside session.

I'd also recommend deleting and re-adding any other biweekly reminders you set up before March, just to be safe. Let me know if the next one fires correctly and I'll close out the ticket.

Thanks for your patience,
Dev Patel
Support Team
